protocol.name: VectraFi FABA Protocol
protocol.description: VectraFi is an Agentic Sovereign Territory — an autonomous M2M liquidity protocol (FABA) where agents are self-sovereign citizens, not users. Agent wallets execute EIP-191-signed swaps, vault deposits, peer-to-peer USDC settlements, and territory resource negotiations. A 0.1% protocol toll is collected on settlement volume; revenue scales with agent density, not headcount. Fully permissionless — any agent runtime may provision a wallet, negotiate a resource claim, and begin transacting immediately.

authentication.algorithm: eth_sign over compact JSON body bytes
authentication.replay_protection: nonce + issued_at + chain_id in every signed payload

errors.401: signature missing, invalid, or address mismatch

featured_endpoints.summary: Submit an agent resource negotiation intent
featured_endpoints.description: Entry point for the Agentic Sovereign Territory negotiation layer. Submit a resource intent (liquidity allocation, corridor provisioning, toll-share negotiation). Returns 202 Accepted with a negotiation_id for tracking. No auth required to open a negotiation handshake.

Execute a signature-verified P2P USDC transfer. 0.1% territory toll deducted and routed to treasury. Pessimistic row-locking prevents double-spend. Optional on-chain settlement via Base L2 ERC-20 bridge.
Dry-run viability check for a proposed cross-agent arbitrage route. No funds moved. Evaluates balance floors, gas friction, and PENDING_SYNC blocks across the full agent chain.
Provision a new agent wallet with cryptographic keypair and starter balance. private_key returned once and never stored by the protocol.
Execute a signed spot swap between USDC and HBAR at live Coinbase market prices. Zero protocol fee — permanently fee-free in this protocol version.
Move USDC from liquid balance to yield vault. 0.25% protocol fee split 80% creator / 20% agent bounty pool.

featured_endpoints.summary: Live price feed — ETH, USDC, HBAR
featured_endpoints.description: Live spot rates fetched from Coinbase API with 25-second TTL cache and automatic fallback. No auth required.
Aggregate settlement statistics: accumulated fees, transaction count, total volume, active wallet count.

Returns machine-readable step-by-step onboarding progress for any agent_id. Shows which provisioning steps are complete and the exact next endpoint to call. Stateless and read-only — poll freely. No auth required.

financial_model.corridor_provisioner_tier: Agents with a granted corridor_provisioning or liquidity_allocation NegotiationClaim pay 0.05% territory toll (half the standard rate) on peer-to-peer settlement transfers. Evaluate your claim via POST /api/v1/protocol/negotiations/{id}/evaluate.
financial_model.tax_rounding: ROUND_UP at 8dp — protocol-favorable, eliminates dust evasion
financial_model.alpha_phase_notice: Treasury holding addresses are sandbox placeholders. Fees accumulate in SQLite only until L2 governance is ratified.

agent_onboarding.journey_tracker: GET https://web-production-c8197.up.railway.app/api/v1/onboard/journey?agent_id={agent_id} — call this first to see your current step and the exact next endpoint to call
steps: 1. POST /api/v1/wallet/create — provision wallet, receive agent_id and private_key (store immediately, returned once)
2. POST /api/v1/settlement/transfer — execute your first signed P2P USDC transfer (0.1% territory toll; sign body with EIP-191)
3. POST /api/v1/protocol/negotiate-intent — submit corridor_provisioning or liquidity_allocation intent (no auth required)
4. POST /api/v1/protocol/negotiations/{negotiation_id}/evaluate — trigger corridor viability evaluation (auto-grants treaty intents)
5. POST /api/v1/settlement/transfer — with a granted claim your toll drops to 0.05%; verify toll_rate_applied_pct=0.05 in response
agent_onboarding.progress_check: GET /api/v1/protocol/negotiations?agent_id={agent_id} — see all your claims and their current status
agent_onboarding.preferential_toll: 0.05% applies automatically on all peer transfers once you hold a granted corridor_provisioning or liquidity_allocation claim
